description: Mystery requires an investigator, a victim, a culprit active throughout, a suspect circle, and clues that all appear before the reveal — fair play and a mid-story pivot are mandatory.

character_constraints.description: The character who drives the investigation — professional or amateur, but must actively pursue the truth.
The character whose fate initiates the mystery — their identity and relationships generate suspects.
The guilty party — must be active and present throughout the story, not introduced late.
Characters with motive, means, or opportunity who could plausibly be guilty.
Characters who provide partial, unreliable, or contradictory testimony.

relationship_constraints.description: The investigator's connection to the victim — personal or professional — that drives the investigation.
Active questioning relationships — the investigator must press suspects and witnesses for information.

place_constraints.description: The location of the crime — sealed or revisitable, containing physical evidence.
A location or social context containing all suspects — country house, office, small town.
The investigator's home base for piecing together evidence — office, study, incident room.

object_constraints.description: Physical or informational evidence that, correctly interpreted, points to the culprit.
Forensic or documentary proof that narrows the suspect pool.
The means of the crime — must be established and consistent.

element_rules.description: All clues necessary to solve the mystery must appear before the reveal — fair play is mandatory.
element_rules.testable_condition: Every clue referenced in the reveal scene appears earlier in the timeline.

element_rules.description: A mid-story pivot that reframes the investigation is mandatory — the initial theory must prove wrong.
element_rules.testable_condition: A 'reframes' or 'discovers' transition appears in the middle third of the timeline that changes the direction of investigation.
